name: Ubiquiti airMAX NanoBeam 5AC
title: Ubiquiti airMAX NanoBeam 5AC
title_extended: Ubiquiti NanoBeam AC Gen2 – 5GHz Point-to-Point Wireless Bridge
short_description: 5GHz, Throughput 450+ Mbps, Range 15+ km, 19dBi Dual Antenna, 1X GLAN, POE-RJ-45, 802.11ac, Wi-Fi for Management
description: 5GHz, Throughput 450+ Mbps, Range 15+ km, 19dBi Dual Antenna, 1X GLAN, POE-RJ-45, 802.11ac, Wi-Fi for Management

detailed_description.heading: Compact 5 GHz WiFi bridge
detailed_description.text: The airMAX NanoBeam 5AC Gen2 represents a significant advancement in Ubiquiti Networks' lineup of compact wireless network bridges, embodying the perfect amalgamation of innovative design and advanced technology to deliver superior wireless broadband connectivity. Designed for point-to-point (PtP) and point-to-multipoint (PtMP) applications, this device is tailored to meet the needs of high-speed, long-distance communication with enhanced efficiency and reliability.

detailed_description.heading: Performance and Hardware
detailed_description.text: The NanoBeam 5AC Gen2 is fully compatible with other devices in the airMAX ac family. This includes a wide range of Ubiquiti's outdoor wireless access points and client devices that also utilize airMAX ac technology. Such compatibility is crucial for setting up efficient and high-capacity point-to-point (PtP) and point-to-multipoint (PtMP) links, ensuring reliable and high-speed wireless connectivity across various deployment scenarios. This device, operating on the 5 GHz frequency band using airMAX ac technology, is geared towards ensuring seamless integration and optimal performance when paired with other Ubiquiti airMAX devices. Please also note, that compatibility with older airMAX devices (those using the 802.11n standard) might be limited. For optimal performance, it is recommended to use devices within the same technological generation.

detailed_description.heading: Connectivity and PoE Features
detailed_description.text: In addition, please keep in mind that NanoBeam 5AC Gen2 does not natively integrate with Ubiquiti's UniFi series. The NanoBeam 5AC Gen2, being part of the airMAX series, operates on a different platform from Ubiquiti's UniFi products. While UniFi devices are managed through the UniFi Controller software and cater to managed Wi-Fi networks, the NanoBeam and other airMAX devices use airOS for configuration and are focused on long-distance outdoor wireless links. Thus, direct integration between airMAX and UniFi devices is not inherent.

detailed_description.heading: Storage, USB, and Value
detailed_description.text: The device also incorporates Power over Ethernet (PoE) functionality, allowing both power and data to be transmitted over a single Ethernet cable. This simplifies installation by eliminating the need for a separate power source, making the NanoBeam 5AC Gen2 easier to deploy in a variety of locations, even those where power outlets are not readily accessible. Security is a key consideration in the design of the NanoBeam 5AC Gen2, featuring WPA2 AES encryption to protect the wireless network from unauthorized access. This level of security ensures that sensitive data transmitted over the network remains secure from potential threats.
